WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE FINAL RULING ON THE WRIT, PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IF ANY ACTUAL CONTROVERSY STILL REMAINS UNRESOLVED. calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Andrew Catterall; Zacks Freedman & Patterson, PC; 415-956-8100; appearing via Zoom on behalf of petitioners/plaintiffs John Farrell and Claudette Farrell.
Carlos Jato; Law Office of Carlos Jato; 415-771-6174; appearing via Zoom on behalf of respondents/defendants Roque A. Hernandez and Eric Black.

Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Plaintiffs contest the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS ROQUE HERNANDEZ and ERIC BLACK's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as follows: action against the moving defendants (cause of action for declaratory relief) shall be stayed until writ is resolved. Within 15 days of the final ruling on the writ, Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint for declaratory relief if any actual controversy still remains unresolved. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROQUE HERNANDEZ, ERIC BLACK DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, per request of the moving party. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (D501)
